bullets: Sabic copo settled $1,200 CIF, $50–60 below this week’s bid.
NSRP 114G down to 33,400–33,600; Sibur homo cut 1,300–1,600.
Korean off-grade copo crashed to 30,500–31,500 VND/kg.
Lotte Titan cut LDPE again — 260GG to $1,500 CIF.
Chinese LLDPE hit a market-low 31,500 VND/kg.
Sibur cut Hai Phong PE 400–700 VND/kg.
Formosa’s July offer landed at $780 CIF, not the $810–820 first assumed.
Sinopec oil-based to $720 — below the $750–800 production cost.
Coal-based SG-5 broke to 21,600; oil-based to 22,500.

editorial.changedNote: 33 of 50 moved down, led by PP copo

summary: Lotte Titan re-offered July PE: LDPE 260GG down $40 to $1,500 CIF, LDPE injection 300YY down $60 to $1,560; HDPE Film $1,280 and blow $1,330 are competitive.
Chinese offers eased: BASF HDPE Film 6095 to $1,215 CNF, Sinopec LDPE 2426H $1,440, Petrochina LLDPE 7042 down $25 to $1,120–1,130 CIF.
Domestic spot under heavy pressure: Qamar HDPE F1 finalized 38,000–38,300, Sabic LLDPE 218WJ at 35,500, and Chinese LLDPE hit a market-low 31,500 VND/kg.
paras: Mid-week, Lotte Titan Malaysia offered July PE import prices to Vietnam: common LDPE Film 260GG/200GG at $1,540/ton CIF, and LLDPE injection 300YY and LDPE coating 800YY both at $1,620/ton CIF. The distributor then sold out and briefly paused offering.

Today, June 10, Lotte Titan re-offered July PE to Vietnam. LDPE grades fell $40/ton vs last week, bringing 260GG to $1,500/ton CIF; LDPE injection 300YY dropped $60/ton to $1,560/ton CIF. The distributor also offered HDPE blow and Film — HDPE Film at $1,280/ton CIF, HDPE blow at $1,330/ton CIF. LDPE offers keep falling, while Titan’s HDPE pricing is quite competitive.

On Chinese goods: last week some suppliers offered BASF China HDPE Film 6095 (unimodal) at $1,255/ton CIF; today a distributor offered it at $1,215/ton CNF Vietnam, down $40/ton. Baofeng China’s HDPE Film 5S110 (bimodal) was offered this week at $1,240/ton CNF.

For LDPE, Sinopec China’s LDPE Film 2426H was offered at $1,440/ton CIF; ZPC’s 2426H/2420H at $1,430/ton CIF; and Exxon-China’s 2022.AC/2022.BW at $1,455/ton CIF. Against last week’s $1,440–1,470 for Yulong/Wanhua 2426H/2420H, the Chinese LDPE level eased slightly.

For LLDPE, a distributor offered Petrochina’s LLDPE Film 7042/7042N at $1,120–1,130/ton CIF, down $25/ton vs last week. Exxon Mobil US LLDPE Film 2018AY (exported from China) was offered at $1,210/ton CIF.
On US goods: Tricon’s HDPE blow 5502 was offered at $1,170/ton CIF and Cynpol’s HDPE blow 5502H at $1,190/ton CIF — overall slightly lower than last week.

On Russian goods at Hai Phong, Sibur offered LLDPE Film at 34,200 VND/kg incl. VAT (down 400), HDPE Film grades at 36,500 (down 500), HDPE yarn at 35,600 (down 700), and HDPE injection at 35,200–35,500 (down 500) versus last week.

With import prices still falling, domestic spot is under strong pressure; many commercial sellers joined a “price-cutting race” to clear inventory rather than hold goods, believing PE prices are hard to reverse and may fall further.

In the North, some sellers cut Lotte Titan LDPE 260GG to 49,000 VND/kg incl. VAT; Middle-Eastern LDPE Film such as Sabic and Borouge was commonly offered down to 48,000. Qamar’s HDPE Film F1 saw a finalized sale at 36,500 VND/kg excl. VAT, while other sellers finalized at 38,000–38,300 incl. VAT, down 1,500–2,000 vs early May.

Sabic’s LLDPE Film 218WJ was offered by many at 35,500 VND/kg incl. VAT — equal to last week’s lowest finalized price (offers had ranged 36,000–37,000 by distributor). Notably, Chinese-origin LLDPE Film recorded the lowest transaction at 31,500 VND/kg incl. VAT.

summary: Chinese offers stay high and scattered: Oriental/Grand Resource $1,285–1,310 FOB, while Jinneng holds the market low at $1,175 FOB Qingdao.
Sabic copo settled at $1,200/ton CIF, $50–60 below this week’s bid; Sibur cut domestic PP 1,300–1,600 VND/kg.
NSRP 114G fell to 33,400–33,600; BSR T3034 to 35,000; Korean off-grade copo collapsed to 30,500–31,500 VND/kg.
paras: On June 9, a distributor offered PP yarn/injection, BOPP and PP Fibre from Oriental and Grand Resource at $1,285–1,310/ton FOB China; Jinneng’s PP yarn HP500N was at $1,200/ton FOB Qingdao; Grand Resource block copo at $1,350–1,355/ton FOB Ningbo. PPR from Oriental, Grand Resource, Chambroad and Sinochem ran $1,315–1,360/ton FOB; Grand Resource IPP/CPP $1,320–1,360/ton FOB. CIF Vietnam typically adds ~$20–30/ton.

Those offers are seen as high versus the general level. Jinneng’s distributor reported PP yarn/injection HP550J/HP500N at $1,175/ton FOB Qingdao and BOPP at $1,185/ton FOB Qingdao for June–July delivery — sideways vs last week but the lowest in the market.
The gap among Chinese suppliers is wide; most Chinese PP offers remain high and not yet competitive.
Sabic’s distributor announced finalized PP copo prices for Saudi-plant shipments (incl. 3% import tax) at $1,200/ton CIF — $50–60/ton below the bid quoted earlier this week.
Sibur offered PP yarn/injection and non-woven at 33,900–34,300 VND/kg incl. VAT by grade (down 1,300–1,600 vs last week), and PP copo at 34,800 VND/kg incl. VAT at Hai Phong (down 700).

Domestically, NSRP’s PP 114G in the North was commonly 33,400–33,600 VND/kg incl. VAT for container-scale volume; one buyer reported a finalized import at 32,000 VND/kg excl. VAT. BSR’s PP T3034 to both South and North fell to 35,000 VND/kg incl. VAT.

On copo/random in the North, a distributor offered Korea Lotte’s random copo J560s at 37,000 VND/kg incl. VAT — down as much as 8,000 vs its own mid-May offer — as Korean off-grade copo dropped to 30,500–31,500 VND/kg incl. VAT.
In the South, PP copo offers ran broadly 36,000–38,000 VND/kg incl. VAT, with buyers signing nearer 36,000 or below. Hyosung Vina’s PP random was offered around 46,000–47,000 VND/kg incl. VAT.

summary: Formosa’s July offer to Vietnam came in at $780/ton CIF Hai Phong (5% tax) — below the $810–820 first assumed — with limited interest.
Sinopec oil-based S-1000 offered at $720 CIF Hai Phong / $735 HCMC, below the $750–800 China production cost (selling at a loss).
Coal-based SG-5 (Erdos) broke to 21,600 VND/kg (21,000 in volume); Chinese oil-based PVC cut to 22,500.
paras: On June 9, Formosa Plastics announced July PVC offers to the region, down $115–120/ton vs last month. As FPC’s prior offer to Vietnam was $930–940/ton CIF, many initially assumed this month’s would fall to about $810–820/ton CIF.

However, on June 10 some Vietnamese customers received the official July figure: $780/ton CIF Hai Phong, subject to 5% import tax. Interest was limited, mainly from refineries or processors already buying other FPC goods/services.

Also today, a major distributor offered Sinopec oil-based PVC S-1000 at $720/ton CIF Hai Phong and $735/ton CIF HCMC (incl. CIC), down ~$100–120/ton vs mid-May, to compete with much cheaper coal-based material.
Market sources put China’s oil-based PVC production cost at about $750–800/ton — meaning the offer above implies selling at a loss.

Domestically in the North, a major distributor holding ~500 tons of coal-based SG-5 (Erdos) offered it at 21,600 VND/kg incl. VAT — 400 below last week’s low — and indicated ~21,000 for large volume, suggesting the coal-based “floor” keeps breaking lower.

Oil-based cuts were sharper: some sellers dropped Chinese-origin oil-based PVC to 22,500 VND/kg incl. VAT, narrowing the gap to coal-based. Other oil-based grades remain under heavy downward pressure.
